depression.description: I often feel like I'm stuck in a cycle with my family where my needs are overlooked, and it leaves me feeling powerless and sad.
When I'm with my family, I find myself zoning out and suppressing my feelings because I fear that expressing them will lead to conflict or rejection.

depression.topic: Loneliness despite being surrounded by family
depression.description: Even when I'm with my family, I feel incredibly lonely and disconnected, as if my emotions and needs are invisible to everyone around me.
I often think that I'm unlovable and that my feelings don't matter, which makes it hard for me to reach out for support when I need it.

Delusion.description: I constantly worry that if I stand up for myself, it will lead to a huge conflict, so I often just go along with what my parents want.

Delusion.topic: Belief that my needs are secondary
Delusion.description: I feel like my feelings and needs are always secondary to my parents', and it makes me feel invalidated and unimportant.

psychosis.description: I believe that my emotional reactions are not valid in my family, which makes me feel ashamed and humiliated when I do express them.
I find myself detaching from my emotions during family interactions because I'm scared that if I let them out, they'll overwhelm me.
